issues: INACCURATE FRAMING: Article states 'In Deutschland gibt es 17 Landesärztekammern, eine je Bundesland (Bayern und Sachsen haben je eine, Hamburg und Berlin je eine)'. The parenthetical lists four states that each have one chamber, which is true of nearly every state and explains nothing. The actual reason the count is 17 rather than 16 is that Nordrhein-Westfalen has TWO chambers (Ärztekammer Nordrhein and Ärztekammer Westfalen-Lippe). The article omits this entirely.
All required sections present. 4 sources, all authoritative official URLs (BÄK, BLÄK, gematik Fachportal).
Technical content on HBA process is accurate. BAN reference and VDA roles are correctly described.
§ 95d SGB V citation for Fortbildungspflicht and 250-Punkte threshold: correct.
articles.fixInstructions: Replace the parenthetical in Überblick: change '(Bayern und Sachsen haben je eine, Hamburg und Berlin je eine)' to '(Nordrhein-Westfalen zählt zwei: Ärztekammer Nordrhein und Ärztekammer Westfalen-Lippe)'. This is the actual reason the count is 17 and not 16.

issues: FACTUAL ERROR: Article states gRPC is 'seit 2016 als Open-Source-Projekt unter der Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) verfügbar'. Both dates are wrong: (1) Google open-sourced gRPC in February 2015 (not 2016). (2) CNCF adopted gRPC in March 2017 (not 2016). Source: Google Open Source Blog announcement February 26, 2015; CNCF announcement March 1, 2017.

BROKEN LINKS: [[SOAP]] appears 3 times throughout the article. No SOAP article exists in the knowledge base. Confirmed by test-report.json (3 broken-link errors). This is the sole failing article in the structural test run.
All required sections present. Table comparing SOAP/REST/gRPC is accurate. Protocol Buffers description is correct.
TI-Gateway and VSDM 2.0 gRPC integration claims are unverifiable from public sources but directionally consistent with gematik TI 2.0 strategy.
articles.fixInstructions: 1. Fix the CNCF/year claim in Überblick: replace 'seit 2016 als Open-Source-Projekt unter der Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) verfügbar' with 'seit Februar 2015 als Open-Source-Projekt von Google veröffentlicht und seit März 2017 unter der Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF)'. 2. Fix broken [[SOAP]] links: either create a stub article at content/standards/SOAP.md (recommended), or replace all three [[SOAP]] occurrences with plain text 'SOAP' until the article exists. All three broken-link errors will be resolved once a SOAP article or alias exists.

issues: FACTUAL ERROR: Article states 'Rechtsgrundlage ist § 25 der Strahlenschutzverordnung (StrlSchV)'. This paragraph number is incorrect. The current StrlSchV 2018 (in force since 1. Januar 2019) regulates Teleradiologie in § 123 StrlSchV. The predecessor was § 3 Abs. 4 RöV (Röntgenverordnung). § 25 StrlSchV governs a different subject matter. The error appears in the Überblick section and in the comparison table in Technische Details.

UNVERIFIABLE CLAIM: '30 Minuten Befundung' as a statutory deadline. The text of § 123 StrlSchV uses 'unmittelbar' and 'sofort' but does not specify a 30-minute limit. No authoritative source for this specific time window was found. Some Landesbehörden tolerate up to 45 minutes, some even 60 minutes according to commentary literature.
EBM-Ziffer 34800 Telekonsil 'seit 2017': verified correct (in force 1. April 2017 per KBV).
All required sections present. 4 sources. Springer and KBV sources are authoritative.
DICOM and workflow descriptions are technically accurate.
articles.fixInstructions: 1. Replace all occurrences of '§ 25 der Strahlenschutzverordnung (StrlSchV)' with '§ 123 der Strahlenschutzverordnung (StrlSchV 2018)'. This affects the Überblick section and the Rechtsgrundlage column of the comparison table in Technische Details. 2. Replace the '30 Minuten' claim with a paraphrase of the actual statutory language: 'Der Teleradiologe muss unmittelbar erreichbar sein und den Befund sofort nach Bildübermittlung erstellen (§ 123 StrlSchV).' Remove the specific 30-minute figure unless a verifiable authoritative source is found. 3. Add a source: https://www.gesetze-im-internet.de/strlschv_2018/__123.html

issues: FACTUAL IMPRECISION: The IBM outage section states that affected services were 'für alle über T-Systems angebundenen Leistungserbringer'. This is incorrect. The IBM Deutschland GmbH sektoraler IDP failure primarily affected insured persons (Versicherte) authenticating via Gesundheits-ID for ePA access. T-Systems runs a separate component (VPN-Zugangsdienst), and their outage on the same day affected healthcare providers' network connectivity — a distinct incident. The article conflates two separate failures into one, incorrectly attributing the IBM IDP effects to T-Systems-connected providers.
Outage time resolved 'gegen 11:00 Uhr': confirmed by borncity.com.
Start time 'ca. 3:05 Uhr': confirmed.
Smoke detector cause in Frankfurt Rechenzentrum: confirmed by secondary sources.
All required sections present. 3 authoritative sources.
articles.fixInstructions: Revise the IBM outage paragraph. Replace 'Betroffene Dienste für alle über T-Systems angebundenen Leistungserbringer: ePA (Zugriff und Berechtigungen), E-Rezept (Erstellen, Einstellen, Einlösen), KIM und VSDM' with a description that accurately reflects which users were affected by the IBM sektoraler IDP: 'Betroffene Dienste für Versicherte, die sich über den IBM-sektoralen IDP authentisierten (insbesondere TK-Versicherte): ePA-Zugriff und Berechtigungssetzung, E-Rezept-Nutzung via Gesundheits-ID, KIM. Parallel bestand am selben Tag eine separate Störung beim T-Systems VPN-Zugangsdienst, die Leistungserbringer betraf.' This separates the two distinct incidents.

keyIssues: gRPC.md: wrong year for open-source launch (2016 stated, correct is 2015) and wrong CNCF adoption year (2016 stated, correct is 2017); plus 3 broken [[SOAP]] wiki-links causing the only structural test failure
Teleradiologie.md: wrong Strahlenschutzverordnung paragraph (§ 25 stated, correct is § 123 StrlSchV 2018 since 1. Januar 2019); 30-minute Befundung claim has no statutory basis in § 123
Landesaerztekammer.md: misleading parenthetical omits the actual reason for 17 chambers (NRW has two: Ärztekammer Nordrhein and Ärztekammer Westfalen-Lippe)
IDP.md: IBM outage description conflates two separate 10.02.2026 incidents — IBM sektoraler IDP (affecting Versicherte/Gesundheits-ID) and T-Systems VPN (affecting Leistungserbringer) are distinct
